name: Advanceable-Sequence Intent
summary: The shared UX vocabulary for a set of discrete, ordered items traversed one position at a time — current/next/prev, timed advance, loop, and an up-next preview. The cross-media kernel that a slide deck, a video player, and a carousel all compose, each adding only its own layer (deck → fragments + presenter; video → scrubbing + buffering; carousel → peek + dots). Factored once in webintents rather than re-specified per consumer; the carousel block already owns the advance/sequence mechanics, so this generalises that kernel. Distinct from navigation (hierarchical/lateral view changes) — this is strictly positional traversal over equivalent ordered items.

advance.description: What drives a move to the next position. manual = user action only (click/key/swipe); timed = an interval auto-advances (autoplay/kiosk); on-complete = the current item signals it is done (a video ends, an animation finishes).

traversal.description: Which directions the sequence can move. forward = next-only (a linear walkthrough); bidirectional = next and prev, with reverse traversal restoring prior state (deck back-nav, video step-back).

boundary.description: What happens at the ends of the sequence. stop = clamp at the last/first item; loop = wrap around to the other end; rewind = on reaching the end, return to the start (autoplay restart).

preview.description: Whether the next item is surfaced ahead of arrival. none = no preview; up-next = a what-comes-next affordance (deck next-slide peek, video playlist up-next, carousel peek) so the user can anticipate or skip ahead.

description:
One family, three consumers

The headline harvest from the deck placement decision (#1175): a deck, a video player, and a carousel are one family. They share advance · timed-autoplay · up-next · present-surface · boundary behavior over a set of discrete, ordered items. Rather than re-specify those per consumer, the Advanceable-Sequence intent factors the shared kernel once; each medium composes it and adds only its own layer.

What each consumer adds

  • Deck — fragments (finer-than-item reveal), 2D addressing, presenter mode.
  • Video — a continuous scrub timeline, buffering, captions.
  • Carousel — peek of adjacent items, dot markers, loop.

Prior art it generalises

The carousel block already owns the advance/sequence mechanics (scroll-snap paging, APG rotation, opt-in autoplay paused on hover/focus, reduced-motion). This intent lifts that kernel to a cross-media contract so the three consumers cite it instead of reinventing it.

Boundary

Strictly positional traversal over equivalent ordered items — distinct from the Navigation intent (hierarchical/lateral/linear view changes with history + URL semantics). A wizard is navigation; a slide deck is an advanceable sequence whose items may each be a view.
